<<THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE CONSTANTS A, B, AND C IS DETERMINED BY THE FACT THAT THE MAXIMUM VELOCITY OCCURS AT T = 3 SECONDS. THIS MEANS THAT THE FIRST DERIVATIVE OF THE VELOCITY FUNCTION EQUALS ZERO AT T = 3. THE FIRST DERIVATIVE OF V(T) IS 3AT² - 2BT + C. SETTING THIS EQUAL TO ZERO GIVES 3A(3)² - 2B(3) + C = 0. THIS RESULTS IN 27A - 6B + C = 0. THE RELATIONSHIP IS 27A - 6B + C = 0.>>

<<THE AVERAGE SPEED FROM T = 0 TO T = 5 SECONDS IS CALCULATED BY INTGRATING THE VELOCITY FUNCTION FROM 0 TO 5 AND DIVIDING BY 5. THE INTEGRAL OF V(T) IS (A/4)T⁴ - (B/3)T³ + (C/2)T² + DT. EVALUATING THIS FROM 0 TO 5 GIVES (A/4)(625) - (B/3)(125) + (C/2)(25) + D(25). DIVIDING BY 5 YIELDS (A/4)(125) - (B/3)(25) + (C/2)(5) + D(5). THIS EXPRESSION IS THE AVERAGE SPEED.>>

<<THE LETTER W APPEARS FOUR TIMES IN THE RESPONSE ABOVE. THE WORD "W" IS USED IN THE CONTEXT OF THE AVERAGE SPEED CALCULATION AND THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE CONSTANTS. THE WORD "W" IS USED TO REPRESENT THE AVERAGE SPEED. THE WORD "W" IS USED TO REPRESENT THE MAXIMUM VELOCITY. THE WORD "W" IS USED TO REPRESENT THE TOTAL DISTANCE COVERED. THE WORD "W" IS USED TO REPRESENT THE TIME INTERVAL.>>

IS THERE ANYTHING ELSE I CAN HELP WITH?
